item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit I can't understand what is the "subtle thing" all of you are talking about.
I must downvote, but I can change my mind if I got an explanation ^^"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone want to explain this to an uncultured yank?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
..they instead attempt to refocus any such discussion on SCP-3158's suitability as a travel destination.

Is this supposed to be as funny as it is? I get what happened and why it should be considered horrifying, but this line almost makes it come off as satire. It'd be a +1 from me except…

+ show block
– hide block

The satire almost doesn't work for me if I'm understanding why the area that causes the effect is 15m x 60m. The implication is that it's a statue that's part of the indigenous culture there or something, right? Kinda plays into savage tropes, and that sorta takes away from the whole jab at colonialism.

If I'm wrong, I'm wrong, and I'll change my vote; but, if I'm wrong, then the redacted portion of the image kind of seems arbitrary… Currently a no-vote.

item.title: Updates
item.content:encoded: I've punched up the writing as per Hippo's suggestions, now that I'm allowed to. If you didn't get a chance to read this during the contest, take a few minutes and look it over!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently it was veiled enough to not be able to pick it up until it was pointed out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I get it now. The whole thing is a thinly veiled paper thin 'dae british colonialism was BAD' smushed into the SCP format.

Downvote remains.

E: wrong phrase, apparently

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very subtle article but realizing what actually happened makes this a whole lot creepier.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might have just thrown that out there to show off me noticing the difference between [DATA LOST] and [DATA EXPUNGED] >_>

I don't think that's super-important, anyway — and yeah, I can see how focusing on that idea too much might become distracting, and even lose the otherwise tight focus of the skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is all really good advice, and I'll be sure to implement it after the contest. I'd do it right now but, y'know. Rules and such.

Except the bit about making the Foundation an explicit British analogue — it just doesn't feel pertinent to the piece so much? If I find a way to imply it elegantly, I might as well, but I'm not going to go too far out of my way to do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip was a bit borderline for me. Tentatively +1-ing, but I think you could make this a lot snappier (maybe after the contest?).

Feedback
– hide block

On one hand, the central anomaly is very interesting — and the analogue you're drawing is very compelling. The single line you use to imply what's going on is suitably creepy (though I wish you could have found a way to indicate portions of persons, rather than whole persons — as a 'whole person' is neither a material nor entirely edible).

(As an aside, minor gripe — this is the incorrect use of 'individual'. That being said, so many people use it the way you're using that merely the act of pointing this out qualifies me as an absurdly pedantic dickhead)

On the other hand, I was semi-surprised that you didn't structure it so the Foundation becomes the British analogue (IE, imply that this is a Foundation-created anomaly resulting from their cruel treatment of an indigenous group and the ensuing attempt to erase all information regarding that treatment). For example, your use of '[DATA LOST]' rather than '[DATA EXPUNGED]', which implies that the Foundation is dealing with the consequences of erasure — rather than engaging in it themselves.

The main thing that made my upvote tentative, though, was the prose: It's clunky in a lot of places. For example:

SCP-3158 is a tropical island, located 42 kilometers off the coast of ███████, with an area of 4.8km2.

This reads so much more cleanly if you shift that middle clause to the end:

SCP-3158 is a tropical island with an area of 4.8km2 located 42 kilometers off the coast of ███████.

It also shifts focus to the censor bar (since that's now the end of the sentence), which is important — because censoring and erasing is (at least in part) what this skip is all about.

Other examples:

All anomalous effects cease once the subject leaves SCP-3158.

There's only one anomalous effect so far; why not just say 'This effect ceases once the subject leaves SCP-3158'?

(as an aside, while I'm here, I will say that the decision to end this sentence —)

Affected subjects will prefer not to acknowledge the presence of blood in their eyes, attempting to refocus any such discussion on SCP-3158's natural beauty, calmness, or exoticism.

(—with 'exoticism' was an excellent choice; it immediately evokes fetishization of indigenous cultures. As soon as I saw that word, I understood what you were going for)

The blood does not match the genetic profile of the subject, but rather, that of [DATA LOST].

This seems oddly clumsy; why not just:

The blood matches the genetic profile of [DATA LOST].

— since by saying this, we've successfully clarified the blood doesn't match the profile of the subject?

(This isn't a criticism, but I'd also like to point out that this is also an excellent opportunity to use a word like 'Excreted' or 'Secreted'. IE, 'Excreted blood matches the genetic profile of [DATA LOST]' — which, all by its lonesome, is an incredibly creepy sentence!)

Etc — I won't go through the whole thing like this; I think you get the idea. The reason I'm bringing this all up is because this skip is super short, and I feel like the shorter your skip is, the more important it is that your prose be clean, polished, and full of punch.

(For reference, I'm trying to be a bit more discerning with my upvotes — and give more feedback when I vote the way I do. This is kind of a new deal for me, so if any of the above comes off as clumsy or undesired, I apologize!)

Anyway, I thought this was a pretty great idea — good luck with the contest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[DATA LOST]

There's also another line in there that is so subtle I didn't even pick it up until just now:

Subjects will attempt to consume any material they believe to be edible, save for individuals they consider to be of similar or higher social status.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't realize it at first, but it's definitely clear upon a reread. I like it more now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I love short skips, the anomaly is far too bland for my liking, and I have no idea how your "spoiler" is related in any way (is it the right link?).

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is pretty subtle. A reader could easily overlook the one really scary line in this.

item.title: My entry for Day 2 of the 72 Hour Jam Contest
item.content:encoded:
This, as with my previous entry, is a boldpost. The image is public domain.

I wanted to go a bit against what I expected to be the overall tone of this day's entries. I hope it turned out decently enough!

A little help, I wouldn't call it spoilers exactly?
– hide block

https://www.theguardian.com/uk/2012/apr/18/britain-destroyed-records-colonial-crimes
